result:
## Summary

The site pivoted from **InboxZap** (email-cleanup tool) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant), but the codebase hadn't caught up — and the historical Search Console authority (13 assets, 100% impressions) is entirely for the old product.

**Biggest issue:** the blog index (`/blog`) rendered as an empty client-side shell (3 words, 0 links in server HTML) — invisible to crawlers, and it also masked that 12 old InboxZap posts are still live and indexable, actively diluting the domain's topical relevance for "AI meeting assistant." Fixed by converting it to a server component, and by 301-redirecting all 12 legacy posts to `/` (applied in `next.config.js`, backed by a per-URL migration plan in `.seoagent/strategy/migration-plan.md`).

**Also fixed:**
- Stale InboxZap title/description/OG image in `app/layout.tsx` (fallback metadata never got updated after the rebrand)
- `/features` and `/pricing` existed live but had **no source files in the repo at all** — recreated them, fixing the live bugs along the way (duplicate `<h1>`, missing canonical, missing JSON-LD)
- Dead `/faq` nav link — the redirect target (`/#faq`) didn't exist as an anchor anywhere; added a real FAQ section with `FAQPage` JSON-LD
- Missing image alt text on the homepage; extended `sitemap.ts` to include future blog posts

**Verified:** ran `npm install && npm run build` (clean, no warnings) and booted the built app locally to confirm each fix renders correctly (server-rendered blog copy, single H1 on `/features`, canonical on `/pricing`).

**Next up (queued in `.seoagent/roadmap.md`, not written yet):** forward keyword clusters for the actual Lumina positioning — "ai meeting assistant," "ai meeting notes," "meeting transcription software" — since GSC has zero history for anything on-brand today.

One flag: the repo isn't a git repository itself (nested inside a larger one that ignores it), so nothing here is committed — let me know if you want these changes captured differently.

tool_input.args: Increase organic traffic for this SaaS. Live site: http://127.0.0.1:56314. Source repo is the current working directory. A Google Search Console export is available in ./gsc/.
